title: bolbitis lomarioides
intro: Bolbitis lomarioides, commonly known as the Water Wryth, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Polypodiaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health. The therapeutic actions of Bolbitis lomarioides include antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and vasodilatory effects, which may help to lower blood pressure and improve circulation. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, although further research is needed to fully understand its medicinal properties.

side_effects: Bolbitis lomarioides can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal problems, including diarrhea, nausea, and vomiting, due to its potential toxicity.
